description: A 40-item assessment measuring four dimensions of emotional intelligence: Self-Awareness, Self-Management, Social Awareness, and Relationship Management.

consentBlock.title: Personality-Based Emotional Intelligence Test
consentBlock.bodyText: This assessment contains 40 statements about how you perceive and manage emotions. For each statement, indicate how accurately it describes you. There are no right or wrong answers. Your responses will be used to generate an emotional intelligence profile as part of your Legacy Portrait. Your data is stored securely and encrypted. This instrument is based on open-source items from the Open Source Psychometrics Project.
consentBlock.requiredCheckboxLabel: I understand this is a self-report emotional intelligence assessment and consent to my responses being stored and scored.

disclaimerText: This assessment is for personal insight and legacy-building purposes only. It is not a clinical diagnostic tool. Based on open-source items from the Open Source Psychometrics Project (openpsychometrics.org).

questions.text: I am aware of my emotions as I experience them.

self_awareness.text: Your Self-Awareness score is low, suggesting you may benefit from paying more attention to your emotional states and what triggers them.
Your Self-Awareness score is average, indicating a reasonable understanding of your own emotions and their causes.
Your Self-Awareness score is high, reflecting a strong ability to recognize and understand your own emotions as they occur.

self_management.text: Your Self-Management score is low, suggesting you may find it challenging to regulate your emotional responses in difficult situations.
Your Self-Management score is average, indicating a reasonable ability to manage your emotions in most situations.
Your Self-Management score is high, reflecting strong emotional regulation skills and the ability to stay composed under pressure.

social_awareness.text: Your Social Awareness score is low, suggesting you may sometimes miss emotional cues from others or find it hard to read social situations.
Your Social Awareness score is average, indicating a reasonable ability to perceive and understand others' emotions.
Your Social Awareness score is high, reflecting strong empathy and an ability to read social dynamics and others' emotional states.

relationship_management.text: Your Relationship Management score is low, suggesting you may find it challenging to navigate emotional aspects of relationships and conflicts.
Your Relationship Management score is average, indicating a reasonable ability to manage relationships and handle emotional interactions.
Your Relationship Management score is high, reflecting strong skills in building rapport, resolving conflicts, and inspiring others.

videoPromptTrigger: Reflect on the emotional situations described in the questions you just answered. Record a brief video sharing how you typically handle emotions in your relationships.

domainDescriptions.self_awareness: Self-Awareness is the foundation of emotional intelligence. It involves recognizing your own emotions as they occur, understanding what triggers them, and knowing how they affect your thoughts and behavior. Research by Goleman and others shows that self-awareness is the cornerstone upon which other emotional intelligence competencies are built, and is strongly linked to effective decision-making and authentic leadership.
domainDescriptions.self_management: Self-Management involves the ability to regulate your emotions, control impulses, and adapt to changing circumstances. It includes managing stress, maintaining motivation, and channeling emotions productively. Research shows that self-management is critical for resilience, goal achievement, and maintaining composure under pressure.
domainDescriptions.social_awareness: Social Awareness involves the ability to understand others' emotions, needs, and concerns. It encompasses empathy, organizational awareness, and the ability to read social dynamics. Research consistently shows that social awareness is essential for effective communication, leadership, and building trust in relationships.
domainDescriptions.relationship_management: Relationship Management involves the ability to inspire, influence, and develop others while managing conflict effectively. It encompasses communication skills, teamwork, and the ability to build and maintain positive relationships. Research shows that relationship management skills are among the strongest predictors of leadership effectiveness and team performance.
domainDescriptions.emotional_recognition: Recognizing Emotions captures the ability to identify and label your emotional states accurately and in real time. High scorers can distinguish between similar emotions (like frustration and disappointment) and understand the nuances of their emotional experience. This skill is foundational to emotional regulation and interpersonal effectiveness.
domainDescriptions.self_assessment: Emotional Triggers awareness reflects understanding of what situations, people, or thoughts activate strong emotional responses. High scorers can anticipate their reactions and prepare accordingly. This self-knowledge helps prevent emotional hijacking and supports more thoughtful responses to challenging situations.
domainDescriptions.emotional_regulation: Emotional Regulation captures the ability to manage and modulate emotional responses appropriately. High scorers can calm themselves when upset, maintain focus under stress, and recover quickly from emotional setbacks. This skill is linked to mental health, relationship quality, and professional effectiveness.
domainDescriptions.adaptability: Adaptability reflects flexibility in handling change and the ability to adjust emotional responses to new situations. High scorers remain effective when facing uncertainty, can shift strategies when needed, and maintain a positive outlook during transitions. Research links adaptability to career success and life satisfaction.
domainDescriptions.empathy: Empathy captures the ability to sense and understand what others are feeling, even when they haven't explicitly expressed it. High scorers are attuned to emotional cues, can take others' perspectives, and respond with appropriate sensitivity. Empathy is considered a core competency for effective leadership, counseling, and conflict resolution.
domainDescriptions.organizational_awareness: Social Cues awareness reflects the ability to read nonverbal communication, group dynamics, and unspoken social norms. High scorers pick up on body language, tone of voice, and contextual signals that others might miss. This skill supports effective navigation of complex social situations.
domainDescriptions.influence: Influence captures the ability to have a positive impact on others through persuasion, inspiration, and effective communication. High scorers can articulate a compelling vision, build consensus, and motivate others toward shared goals. This skill is central to leadership, negotiation, and collaborative problem-solving.
domainDescriptions.conflict_management: Conflict Resolution reflects the ability to navigate disagreements constructively and find mutually beneficial solutions. High scorers can de-escalate tensions, facilitate difficult conversations, and help parties find common ground. Research links this skill to team effectiveness, relationship longevity, and organizational health.
